articles.title: राजस्थान में प्रचंड गर्मी का कहर, बाड़मेर में पारा 45.4 डिग्री के पार; कई जिलों में येलो अलर्ट जारी - DD News
articles.search_term: ("पारा चढ़ा" OR "पारा पहुंचा" OR तापमान OR "तापमान बढ़ा" OR "रिकॉर्ड तोड़ गर्मी" OR "डिग्री सेल्सियस" OR "पारा उबला" OR "अधिकतम तापमान" OR "तापमान 40 पार" OR "तापमान 45 पार" OR "तापमान बढ़ने की संभावना") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
राजस्थान में गर्मी का प्रकोप और तेज हो गया है, जहां कई जिलों में दिन और रात के तापमान में उल्लेखनीय वृद्धि दर्ज की गई है। भारत मौसम विज्ञान विभाग (आईएमडी) ने सोमवार और मंगलवार के लिए राज्य के 19 जिलों में येलो अलर्ट जारी किया है। विभाग ने चेतावनी दी है कि कई क्षेत्रों में अधिकतम तापमान 42 से 45 डिग्री सेल्सियस के बीच रहने की संभावना है।
राजस्थान में राज्य का अधिकतम तापमान सबसे अधिक बाड़मेर में 45.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया, जबकि सिरोही में न्यूनतम तापमान 22.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, इस सप्ताह गर्मी और बढ़ने की संभावना है, और केवल पश्चिमी राजस्थान ही नहीं बल्कि राज्य के उत्तरी और पूर्वी हिस्सों में भी तापमान में वृद्धि देखने को मिल सकती है।
रविवार को राजस्थान भर में आसमान साफ रहा और तेज धूप बनी रही। बाड़मेर के अलावा कई शहरों में भी अत्यधिक तापमान दर्ज किया गया। बीकानेर में 45 डिग्री, जैसलमेर में 44.9 डिग्री, फलोदी में 44.8 डिग्री, चित्तौड़गढ़ में 44.6 डिग्री, कोटा और वनस्थली (टोंक) में 44.2 डिग्री, श्रीगंगानगर में 44.1 डिग्री, जोधपुर में 43.8 डिग्री और चूरू में 43.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस तापमान दर्ज किया गया।
गर्मी का असर अब रात के तापमान पर भी दिखने लगा है। हनुमानगढ़ में राज्य की सबसे गर्म रात दर्ज हुई, जहां न्यूनतम तापमान 33.2 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा। चित्तौड़गढ़ में 32.6 डिग्री, फलोदी में 32.2 डिग्री, बाड़मेर में 32 डिग्री, जोधपुर में 31.1 डिग्री, कोटा में 30.1 डिग्री और जैसलमेर में 30 डिग्री सेल्सियस न्यूनतम तापमान दर्ज किया गया।
मौसम वैज्ञानिकों का कहना है कि रात के समय भी तापमान का अधिक रहना असुविधा बढ़ा रहा है और लोगों को दिन की गर्मी से राहत नहीं मिल पा रही है। जयपुर में रविवार को सुबह से शाम तक तेज धूप रही और लगातार तीसरे दिन तापमान में बढ़ोतरी दर्ज की गई।
राजधानी जयपुर में अधिकतम तापमान 1.3 डिग्री बढ़कर 42.7 डिग्री सेल्सियस पहुंच गया, जबकि न्यूनतम तापमान 28.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया। मौसम विभाग ने अगले 3–4 दिनों में जयपुर में इसी तरह की स्थिति रहने और तापमान में 1 से 2 डिग्री और बढ़ोतरी की संभावना जताई है।
कोटा में भी रविवार को भीषण गर्मी बनी रही, जहां अधिकतम तापमान 44.2 डिग्री और न्यूनतम 30.1 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा। दोपहर 11 बजे से 4 बजे के बीच सड़कों पर आवाजाही काफी कम हो गई और शहर लगभग सुनसान नजर आया। कई गौशालाओं में गायों को गर्मी से बचाने के लिए कूलर लगाए गए हैं और दिन में कई बार पानी का छिड़काव किया जा रहा है।
अजमेर में भी लू चलने के कारण भीषण गर्मी रही। यहां अधिकतम तापमान 42.4 डिग्री से ऊपर दर्ज किया गया, जबकि न्यूनतम तापमान लगभग 27 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा। जोधपुर में तापमान सामान्य से अधिक रहा और अधिकतम तापमान 43.8 डिग्री दर्ज किया गया, जो सामान्य से 2.3 डिग्री अधिक था। मौसम विभाग ने लोगों को दोपहर के समय धूप से बचने, पर्याप्त पानी पीने और हीटवेव से बचाव के उपाय अपनाने की सलाह दी है, क्योंकि आने वाले दिनों में गर्मी का प्रकोप जारी रहने की संभावना है।(इनपुट-आईएएनएस)

articles.title: Rajasthan Sizzles: Mercury Soars Amid Severe Heatwave - Devdiscourse
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan Sizzles: Mercury Soars Amid Severe Heatwave
Rajasthan is experiencing intense heat with temperatures averaging between 42 and 45 degrees Celsius. Barmer reported the state's highest temperature of 45.4 degrees. The Meteorological Department anticipates ongoing dry conditions and predicts heatwaves in certain areas, advising residents to take precautions against heat exposure.
Rajasthan is grappling with an intense heatwave, as maximum temperatures hover between 42 and 45 degrees Celsius, the Meteorological Department reported on Monday. Barmer topped the state's heat chart at 45.4 degrees Celsius, underscoring the prevailing severe heat conditions.
The Jaipur Meteorological Centre has predicted that the entirety of Rajasthan will continue to experience dry weather throughout the week. Isolated heatwave conditions are forecast for the border regions of western Rajasthan and parts of the southeastern areas.
Strong, dusty surface winds at speeds of 20-30 kmph are anticipated over the western, northern, and eastern parts over the next four to five days. Authorities have issued advisories urging residents to take necessary precautions against potential heat exposure, as the mercury is expected to remain elevated.
(With inputs from agencies.)

articles.title: Cows die in agony amid drought in Barmer: Villagers say thousands of people thirsty as temperatures touch 4... - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Cows die in agony amid drought in Barmer:Villagers say thousands of people thirsty as temperatures touch 45°C and canal runs dry
- Copy link
The situation in Rajasthan’s desert district of Barmer is worsening amid extreme heat conditions. In Derasar village, locals claim that more than 10 cows have died in recent days due to severe water scarcity. The presence of cattle carcasses near residential areas has also raised fears of disease spreading in the village.
Villagers said even people are struggling for every drop of water. The canal supplying water to the village has dried up. After videos showing scattered cow carcasses surfaced, the administration sent water tankers to the area.
Barmer district has recorded temperatures above 40°C continuously for the past 20 days. The highest temperature recorded so far this season is 48.3°C.
1-month canal closure worsens crisis
Derasar village has a population of around 3,000 people. Water is mainly supplied through the Barmer Lift Canal, while hand pumps serve as an additional source.
However, the canal has remained shut for nearly a month, worsening the water shortage. Villagers said water was supplied intermittently during the closure period, but it was far below the required demand.
Residents alleged that thousands of people have been facing acute water scarcity for the past 10 days. They also claimed that the PHED department had not sent water tankers until Monday morning.
Villagers claim cows died searching for water
A video from Ramdiyon Ki Basti in Derasar village has surfaced showing carcasses of cows lying around a village water pond. Villagers claimed the animals died due to lack of water.
The body of one cow was found inside the dried-up pond itself. Residents said the cow had come to the pond searching for water, but after failing to find any, it died of thirst while struggling inside the dry reservoir.
Villagers say complaints were ignored
Villager Aziz Khan said he had visited the PHED office in Ramsar nearly 10 days ago and informed officials about the crisis. “Officials assured us that a tanker would be sent, but no tanker had arrived even by Sunday,” he alleged.
Village Sarpanch administrator Arshad Rajad said the suspension of canal water supply had created a severe shortage in the area. “Groundwater sources have dried up. Six cows have died in the village. The administration should also make arrangements for water for animals,” he said.
Officials respond to water crisis
Ramlal Meena, SDM, said instructions had been issued to the Tehsildar to submit a report on the cattle deaths in Derasar village by Monday evening. He added that water tankers had been arranged after discussions with officials from the Water Supply Department.
Hajiraram Balwa, Superintending Engineer (SE), said water supply arrangements had been initiated after receiving complaints from villagers. He said 37 villages in Ramsar block, including Derasar, are connected to the Barmer Lift Canal. However, regular supply was disrupted because of the month-long canal closure.
Dashrath Singh, Village Development Officer (VDO), confirmed that the village was facing a severe water crisis.“Villagers sent me a video showing cows dying due to lack of water. The footage is from yesterday. Officials have been informed,” he said.
Barmer continues to reel under extreme heat
Western Rajasthan remains in the grip of intense heat, with Barmer among the worst-affected districts. Meteorological data shows that Barmer recorded its highest early-May temperature in 31 years this season.
Even during the past 24 hours, temperatures remained close to 45°C. So far this season, Barmer has also remained the hottest district in the country for three consecutive days.
